definition: Use a structural alignment metric such as TM-score as a direct similarity, or convert RMSD/contact-map distance to a bounded similarity. This axis audits whether model performance depends on structural neighbors in the training set, even when sequence identity is low.
example_usage: python metric_to_similarity.py --input tm_scores.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --metric-col tm_score --metric-kind similarity

citations.title: TM-align: a protein structure alignment algorithm based on the TM-score

evidence.summary: TM-align uses TM-score for protein structure alignment and reports fold-level structural similarity.
The paper explains why TM-score balances alignment coverage and accuracy better than raw RMSD for global topology.
TM-align is used for large-scale sequence-independent structure comparisons and fold clustering.

limitations: Requires precomputed structure alignments or reliable predicted structures.
TM-score, RMSD, contact-map overlap, and fold labels answer different biological questions.
High structural similarity does not guarantee functional similarity.
quality_gates: Report the structural alignment tool, normalization convention, and any sequence-identity filters.
Prefer TM-score for global topology and RMSD/contact overlap for local geometry when justified.
Run sequence-identity and structure-similarity curves separately when both inputs exist.
